plan_summary.plan_name: Hauts-de-France 9 GW Sovereign AI Datacenter Campus
plan_summary.primary_goal: Complete Phase 1 (500 MW–1 GW) of the Hauts-de-France hyperscale AI campus within 36 months at PUE ≤1.20, while securing a binding RTE allocation and 60% tenant take-or-pay commitment for the 1–3 GW Phase 2 expansion before Phase 1 construction FID.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the Phase 1 budget surplus against baseline CAPEX plus brownfield remediation plus the buffer-land 18-month holding cost; the Phase 2 FID tenant-commitment margin against the 60% take-or-pay threshold; and the Phase 1 PUE margin against the 1.20 operational ceiling. RTE binding 3 GW allocation, DGSI/ANSSI sovereign-partition signoff, judicial review of the 80% buffer model, and social license on local workforce and heat-reuse are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: Phase 1 CAPEX ceiling
key_values.comment: Upper bound of the €5–10B Phase 1 CAPEX envelope; financial ceiling against which baseline CAPEX, remediation, and buffer-land holding must clear.
key_values.source_text: Phase 1 budget maximum (500 MW-1 GW): €10B — input for initial CAPEX.

key_values.label: Phase 2 FID tenant commitment threshold
key_values.comment: Below this take-or-pay commitment for the 1–3 GW expansion tranche the Phase 2 FID is blocked; the central gate of the Pragmatic Scale-Up strategy.
key_values.source_text: Minimum take-or-pay commitment threshold for 1-3 GW expansion gate to 60% — tenant lock-in gate.

key_values.label: Phase 1 operational PUE ceiling
key_values.comment: Maximum acceptable Phase 1 PUE under liquid-cooling and dry-cooling mandate; exceeding it triggers tenant SLA escalator clauses and risks Phase 2 expansion block.
key_values.source_text: Projected Maximum Operational PUE: 1.20 (includes BESS losses, liquid cooling mandate).

key_values.label: Non-buildable buffer-land area
key_values.comment: 80% of the 161 km² footprint held as ecological offset and expansion reserve; carries an uncosted holding burden flagged by Review Issue 2.

key_values.label: Buffer-land holding period covered by Phase 1 budget
key_values.comment: Review Issue 2 mandates the 18-month upfront ring-fenced holding-cost line item; after that the Expansion Reserve subset would convert to annual renewal.
key_values.source_text: Explicitly budget holding costs for the 128.8 km² buffer for 18 months upfront, ring-fenced from infrastructure CAPEX.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Phase 1 baseline CAPEX excluding land holding and brownfield remediation
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to test whether the €10B Phase 1 ceiling absorbs the uncosted buffer-land holding (Review Issue 2) plus brownfield remediation (Risk 9) on top of base build cost.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bottom-up: liquid-cooling fit-out + substations + civil works + hardware (70% hedged) over 500 MW–1 GW; cross-check against the €5–10B envelope and the implicit €5–10B/GW industry benchmark.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Actual brownfield remediation cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Risk 9 names a €250–750M baseline that could double or triple to €500M–€2.25B if hazardous materials require advanced handling; the gap is the dominant Phase 0/1 CAPEX shock.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Phase 0 comprehensive environmental testing on Cambrai/Valenciennes/Dunkirk parcels; fixed-price contracts with regional specialists once land assembly is finalised.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Annual buffer-land holding cost per hectare
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Review Issue 2 names a €50k–75k/ha/year estimate; undershooting by €20k/ha implies €1.3B early CAPEX overrun. Required to materialise the Phase 1 buffer-holding line item.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Per Data Item 2: local cadastral charges plus maintenance estimates for non-buildable industrial/agricultural land in Hauts-de-France; validate against Land Use Lawyer's regional benchmarks.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved Phase 2 take-or-pay tenant commitment by Phase 1 FID
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below the 60% threshold the Phase 2 FID is blocked; this is the central viability test of the Pragmatic Scale-Up strategy.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Project LOI-to-take-or-pay conversion velocity (Selected Scenario missings) applied to the AI compute demand pipeline in EMEA; sensitivity to tenant credit ratings and SLA preferences.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved operational PUE at Phase 1 1 GW load
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Risk 2 flags a 0.10 PUE swing as €5–10M/GW/year cost impact and potential SLA breach; this margin directly gates anchor-tenant satisfaction.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Digital-twin validation of liquid-cooling under brownfield-deployment conditions; benchmark against existing French liquid-cooled hyperscale sites operating at 1.15–1.25.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Annual buffer-land holding cost
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Converts the per-hectare rate × 128.8 km² × 100 ha/km² into an annual EUR burden, the intermediate step for the 18-month upfront line item required by Review Issue 2.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Buffer-land holding cost over the 18-month Phase 1 ring-fence window
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Materialises the €1.3B-class buffer-holding line item that Review Issue 2 says must be ring-fenced from infrastructure CAPEX; flows directly into the Phase 1 surplus test.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Phase 1 CAPEX surplus after baseline build, remediation, and buffer holding
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the €10B Phase 1 ceiling absorbs baseline build PLUS the two uncosted shock items (remediation, buffer holding) flagged repeatedly in the Risk Register and Review Issue 2.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Phase 2 FID tenant-commitment margin
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin unlocks Phase 2 expansion FID and the path to 9 GW; negative margin strands the €1B–€2B Phase 0/1 capital flagged in Risk 3.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Phase 1 PUE margin against the 1.20 ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin means Phase 1 holds PUE at or below 1.20; negative margin triggers Risk 2 tenant SLA escalators and threatens Phase 2 tenant retention.

unmodelled_gates.label: RTE issues a binding 3 GW transmission allocation before Phase 1 construction FID
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 3 and Review Issue 1 name this as the keystone external dependency; without a binding RTE contract the Phase 2 expansion cannot be financed and Phase 1 capital is at risk of stranding.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Stranded €1B–€2B Phase 0/1 capital if tenants withdraw; the 9 GW scaling pathway is closed regardless of tenant commitment or operational performance.

unmodelled_gates.label: DGSI/ANSSI signoff for sovereign AI partition by Q1 2027
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 5 and the Selected Scenario gates name DGSI/ANSSI signoff as binary; without it the premium domestic revenue stream (€10–20B long-term) is lost and the 60% Phase 2 commitment threshold becomes unreachable.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Loss of €10–20B premium domestic revenue stream per Risk 5; tenant pipeline narrows to non-classified commercial only, depressing the Phase 2 commitment margin.

unmodelled_gates.label: No successful judicial challenge to the hyper-dense 80% buffer model
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 7 names a High/High likelihood-severity for a judicial appeal cutting buildable area from 30 km² to <20 km²; that constrains the 9 GW target to 3–4 GW and forces a fundamental business-case re-evaluation.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Prefecture-mandated buildable-area reduction to <20 km²; full 9 GW target is capped at 3–4 GW; long-term NPV collapses by 30–40% per Review Issue 2 sensitivity.

unmodelled_gates.label: Local social license sustained via 75% local workforce mandate and heat-reuse contracts
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: The Mitigation Plan ties social license to immediate skills-academy funding and heat-reuse feasibility; if these fail, the buffer-permit pathway and the workforce mandate trigger political backlash that the simulation cannot resolve.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Prefecture-mandated footprint reduction; Phase 2 expansion permits stall; the workforce premium overshoots Review Issue 3's 5% modeling assumption and erodes the Phase 1 schedule by 6–12 months.
